51.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever kills a person under a treaty unjustly, Allah will forbid him from smelling the fragrance of Paradise.'
51.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa membunuh seseorang yang berada dalam perjanjian secara tidak benar, Allah akan mengharamkannya mencium wangi surga.'

52.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'What do you think if Juhayna, Aslam, Ghifar, and Muzayna are better with Allah than Banu Asad, Banu Tamim, Banu 'Abdillah ibn Ghatan, and Banu 'Amir ibn Sa'sa'a?' A man said: 'They have failed and lost.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'By the One in Whose hand is my soul, they are better than them.'
52.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Bagaimana pendapat kalian jika Juhaynah, Aslam, Ghifar, dan Muzaynah lebih baik di sisi Allah daripada Bani Asad, Bani Tamim, Bani 'Abdillah ibn Ghatan, dan Bani 'Amir ibn Sa'sa'ah?' Seorang lelaki berkata: 'Mereka telah gagal dan rugi.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Demi Dzat yang jiwaku di tangan-Nya, mereka lebih baik dari mereka.'

53.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'We were sitting with the Prophet ﷺ and he said: "Shall I not inform you of the greatest of major sins?" three times. "Associating partners with Allah, the Mighty and Majestic." The major sins were mentioned before the Prophet ﷺ and he said: "Associating partners with Allah and disobedience to parents." He was reclining, then he sat up and said: "And giving false testimony, and giving false testimony" — and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ kept repeating it until we wished he would be silent.'
53.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami sedang duduk bersama Nabi ﷺ dan beliau bersabda: "Maukah aku beritahu kalian dosa-dosa besar yang paling besar?" tiga kali. "Menyekutukan Allah Yang Maha Perkasa." Dosa-dosa besar disebutkan di hadapan Nabi ﷺ dan beliau bersabda: "Menyekutukan Allah dan durhaka kepada orang tua." Beliau sedang bersandar lalu duduk dan bersabda: "Dan kesaksian palsu, dan kesaksian palsu" — dan Rasulullah ﷺ terus mengulanginya hingga kami berharap beliau diam.'

54.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ delivered a sermon during his Farewell Pilgrimage and said: 'Indeed time has come full circle to its state as on the day Allah created the heavens and the earth. The year is twelve months, four of which are sacred: three consecutive — Dhu al-Qa'da, Dhu al-Hijja, and al-Muharram — and Rajab Mudar, which falls between Jumada and Sha'ban. What day is this?' We said: it is the Day of Sacrifice. He said: 'What month is this?' and we were silent, thinking he would call it by another name. Then he said: 'Is it not the sacred month?'
54.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkhutbah pada haji wada' dan bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya zaman telah berputar kembali seperti keadaannya pada hari Allah menciptakan langit dan bumi. Setahun ada dua belas bulan, empat di antaranya adalah bulan haram: tiga berturut-turut — Dzulqa'dah, Dzulhijjah, dan Muharram — dan Rajab Mudhar yang jatuh antara Jumada dan Sya'ban. Hari apa ini?' Kami menjawab: Hari Nahr. Beliau bertanya: 'Bulan apa ini?' Kami diam mengira beliau akan menyebutnya dengan nama lain. Kemudian beliau bersabda: 'Bukankah ini bulan yang haram?'

55.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ led some in two rak'ahs and others in two rak'ahs, so the Prophet ﷺ prayed four rak'ahs and they each prayed two rak'ahs.
55.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ mengimami sebagian orang dua rakaat dan sebagian lain dua rakaat, sehingga Nabi ﷺ shalat empat rakaat sedangkan mereka masing-masing dua rakaat.'

56.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ delivered a sermon on the day of sacrifice while on a she-camel, and he kept speaking here and there, addressing each group. He then said: 'What day is this?' We fell silent, thinking he would call it by another name. He said: 'Is it not the Day of Sacrifice?' We said: 'Yes.' He then said: 'What month is this?' and we fell silent thinking he would give it another name.
56.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkhutbah pada hari Nahr di atas untanya, dan beliau berbicara ke sini dan ke sana, menyapa setiap kelompok. Kemudian beliau bertanya: 'Hari apa ini?' Kami diam mengira beliau akan menyebutnya dengan nama lain. Beliau bersabda: 'Bukankah ini hari Nahr?' Kami berkata: 'Ya.' Kemudian beliau bertanya: 'Bulan apa ini?' Kami diam mengira beliau akan memberinya nama lain.'

57.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Your blood, your wealth, and your honor are sacred to you, as sacred as this day of yours in this month of yours in this city of yours. Let those present convey this to those who are absent, for perhaps a person who receives this message understands it better than those who heard it directly.'
57.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Darah, harta, dan kehormatan kalian adalah suci bagi kalian, seperti sucinya hari ini, bulan ini, dan kota ini. Hendaklah yang hadir menyampaikan kepada yang tidak hadir, karena mungkin orang yang menerima pesan ini memahaminya lebih baik dari yang mendengarnya langsung.'

58.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The two months of Eid do not diminish — Ramadan and Dhu al-Hijja.'
58.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Dua bulan Eid tidak berkurang — Ramadan dan Dzulhijjah.'

59.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said something regarding the Battle of the Camel, and he mentioned that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had prophesied its occurrence.
59.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda sesuatu tentang Perang Jamal, dan ia menyebut bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ telah meramalkan terjadinya peristiwa itu.'

60.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ came upon some people who were passing a drawn sword between them. He ﷺ said: 'May Allah curse the one who did this. Did I not forbid this?' Then he said: 'When one of you draws his sword and wants to hand it to his brother, let him sheathe it first.'
60.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ menemui orang-orang yang sedang saling mengoper pedang yang terhunus. Beliau ﷺ bersabda: 'Semoga Allah melaknat orang yang melakukan ini. Bukankah aku telah melarang hal ini?' Kemudian beliau bersabda: 'Jika salah seorang dari kalian mencabut pedangnya dan ingin memberikannya kepada saudaranya, hendaklah ia masukkan kembali ke sarungnya terlebih dahulu.'

61.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the major sins were mentioned before the Prophet ﷺ and he said: 'Associating partners with Allah, Blessed and Exalted, and disobedience to parents.' He was reclining, then he sat up and said: 'And false testimony, and false testimony' — and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ kept repeating it until we wished he would be silent.
61.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dosa-dosa besar disebutkan di hadapan Nabi ﷺ dan beliau bersabda: 'Menyekutukan Allah Yang Maha Berkah dan Maha Tinggi, dan durhaka kepada orang tua.' Beliau sedang bersandar lalu duduk dan bersabda: 'Dan kesaksian palsu, dan kesaksian palsu' — dan Rasulullah ﷺ terus mengulanginya hingga kami berharap beliau diam.'

62.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ forbade us from exchanging silver for silver and gold for gold except equal for equal, but he commanded us to exchange silver for gold and gold for silver however we wished. Thabit ibn 'Ubayd asked: 'Hand to hand?' He said: 'That is how I heard it.'
62.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melarang kami menukar perak dengan perak dan emas dengan emas kecuali sama rata, tetapi beliau memerintahkan kami menukar perak dengan emas dan emas dengan perak sesuka kami. Tsabit ibn 'Ubayd bertanya: 'Langsung dari tangan ke tangan?' Ia berkata: 'Begitulah yang aku dengar.'

63.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sa'd that he said: 'My ears heard and my heart retained that whoever claims a lineage to other than his father knowing that he is not his father, Paradise is forbidden for him.' He said: 'I then met Abu Bakra and told him, and he said: And my ears also heard and my heart retained it from Muhammad ﷺ.'
63.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sa'd bahwa ia berkata: 'Kedua telingaku mendengar dan hatiku menyimpan bahwa barangsiapa mengaku-ngaku kepada selain ayahnya padahal ia tahu bahwa itu bukan ayahnya, maka surga haram baginya.' Ia berkata: 'Aku kemudian bertemu Abu Bakra dan memberitahunya, dan ia berkata: Kedua telingaku juga mendengar dan hatiku menyimpannya dari Muhammad ﷺ.'

64.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever kills a person under a treaty unjustly, Allah, Blessed and Exalted, will forbid him Paradise — he will not smell its fragrance.'
64.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa membunuh seorang yang berada dalam perjanjian secara tidak benar, Allah Yang Maha Berkah lagi Maha Tinggi akan mengharamkan surga baginya — ia tidak akan mencium wanginya.'

65.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'There is no sin more deserving of Allah, Blessed and Exalted, hastening the punishment of its perpetrator in this world — along with what He stores for him in the Hereafter — than transgression and severing ties of kinship.'
65.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidak ada dosa yang lebih layak bagi Allah Yang Maha Berkah lagi Maha Tinggi untuk mempercepat hukumannya bagi pelakunya di dunia — beserta apa yang disimpan untuknya di akhirat — selain kezaliman dan memutus silaturahmi.'

66.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) — he believed it was from the Prophet ﷺ — that he said: 'Two months do not diminish — the two months of Eid: Ramadan and Dhu al-Hijja.'
66.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) — ia meyakini itu dari Nabi ﷺ — bahwa beliau bersabda: 'Dua bulan tidak berkurang — dua bulan Eid: Ramadan dan Dzulhijjah.'

67.iwh_en: It was narrated from the father of 'Uyayna ibn 'Abd al-Rahman that he said: 'I attended the funeral of 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Samura. Some men from his family came forward to meet the funeral procession and walked backwards, saying: "Slowly, may Allah bless you." Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) caught up with us from the direction of al-Mirbad, and when he saw them and what they were doing, he charged at them on his mule and raised his whip at them, saying: "Move away! By the One Who honored the face of Abu al-Qasim ﷺ, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not do this."'
67.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari ayah 'Uyaynah ibn 'Abd al-Rahman bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku menghadiri jenazah 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Samurah. Beberapa orang dari keluarganya maju menyambut iring-iringan jenazah dengan berjalan mundur sambil berkata: "Pelan-pelan, semoga Allah memberkahi kalian." Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) menyusul kami dari arah al-Mirbad, dan ketika melihat mereka dan apa yang mereka lakukan, ia mengejar mereka dengan bagalnya dan mengayunkan cambuknya kepada mereka, berkata: "Minggirlah! Demi Dzat yang memuliakan wajah Abu al-Qasim ﷺ, Rasulullah ﷺ tidak melakukan ini."'

68.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The Dajjal is blind in his left eye, and between his eyes is written "Kafir" — which both the literate and the illiterate can read.'
68.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Dajjal buta pada mata kirinya, dan di antara kedua matanya tertulis "Kafir" — yang dapat dibaca oleh orang yang buta huruf maupun yang melek huruf.'

69.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'A people who entrust their affairs to a woman will never succeed.'
69.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Suatu kaum yang menyerahkan urusan mereka kepada seorang wanita tidak akan pernah berhasil.'

70.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever kills a person under a treaty unjustly, Allah will forbid him from smelling the fragrance of Paradise.'
70.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa membunuh seorang yang berada dalam perjanjian secara tidak benar, Allah akan mengharamkannya mencium wangi surga.'

71.iwh_en: It was narrated from the father of 'Uyayna that he said: 'Laylat al-Qadr was mentioned to Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) and he said: I will not seek it except in the last ten nights, after something I heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. I heard him say: "Seek it in the last ten nights — on the ninth remaining, or the seventh remaining, or the fifth remaining, or the third remaining, or the last night."'
71.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari ayah 'Uyaynah bahwa ia berkata: 'Lailatul Qadar disebutkan kepada Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dan ia berkata: Aku tidak akan mencarinya kecuali pada sepuluh malam terakhir, setelah apa yang aku dengar dari Rasulullah ﷺ. Aku mendengar beliau bersabda: "Carilah ia pada sepuluh malam terakhir — pada malam kesembilan yang tersisa, atau ketujuh yang tersisa, atau kelima yang tersisa, atau ketiga yang tersisa, atau malam terakhir."'

72.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he bowed before reaching the row. The Prophet ﷺ said to him: 'May Allah increase your eagerness — but do not repeat this.'
72.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia ruku' sebelum mencapai shaf. Nabi ﷺ berkata kepadanya: 'Semoga Allah menambah semangatmu — tetapi jangan ulangi ini.'

73.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'None of you should say: I stood in prayer throughout Ramadan and I fasted all of it.' He said: 'I do not know whether he disliked self-praise, or because there is inevitably some heedlessness or sleep.'
73.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah salah seorang dari kalian berkata: Aku shalat malam sepanjang Ramadan dan aku berpuasa semuanya.' Ia berkata: 'Aku tidak tahu apakah beliau tidak suka dengan pujian diri, atau karena pasti ada kelalaian atau tidur.'

74.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ addressed the people at Mina and said: 'Do you not know what day this is?' We said: 'Allah and His Messenger know best.' He said: 'Is this not the sacred day?' Then he said: 'Do you not know what city this is?' We said: 'Allah and His Messenger know best.' He said: 'Is this not the sacred city?'
74.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkhutbah kepada manusia di Mina dan bersabda: 'Apakah kalian tidak tahu hari apa ini?' Kami berkata: 'Allah dan Rasul-Nya lebih mengetahui.' Beliau bersabda: 'Bukankah ini hari yang suci?' Kemudian beliau bersabda: 'Apakah kalian tidak tahu kota apa ini?' Kami berkata: 'Allah dan Rasul-Nya lebih mengetahui.' Beliau bersabda: 'Bukankah ini kota yang suci?'

75.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ prayed two rak'ahs with one group and two rak'ahs with another group, so the prayer counted as four rak'ahs for the Prophet ﷺ and two rak'ahs each for them.
75.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ shalat dua rakaat bersama satu kelompok dan dua rakaat bersama kelompok lain, sehingga shalat itu terhitung empat rakaat bagi Nabi ﷺ dan dua rakaat masing-masing bagi mereka.'

76.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ used to say at the end of every prayer: 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from disbelief, poverty, and the punishment of the grave.'
76.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa mengucapkan di akhir setiap shalat: 'Ya Allah, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari kekufuran, kemiskinan, dan azab kubur.'

77.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'What do you think if Juhayna, Aslam, and Ghifar are better than Banu Tamim, Banu 'Abdillah ibn Ghatan, and Banu 'Amir ibn Sa'sa'a?' — and he raised his voice with it. They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, they have failed and lost.' He said: 'By the One in Whose hand is my soul, they are better than them.'
77.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Bagaimana pendapat kalian jika Juhaynah, Aslam, dan Ghifar lebih baik dari Bani Tamim, Bani 'Abdillah ibn Ghatan, dan Bani 'Amir ibn Sa'sa'ah?' — dan beliau mengeraskan suaranya. Mereka berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, mereka telah gagal dan rugi.' Beliau bersabda: 'Demi Dzat yang jiwaku di tangan-Nya, mereka lebih baik dari mereka.'

78.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was walking with the Prophet ﷺ and he passed by two graves. He said: "Who will bring me a fresh palm branch?" I raced with another man and we brought a palm branch. He split it in two and placed one piece on each grave, then said: "Perhaps their punishment will be lightened as long as these remain moist." Then he said: "They are being punished, and they are not being punished for something great" — yet it is great.'
78.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berjalan bersama Nabi ﷺ dan beliau melewati dua kuburan. Beliau bersabda: "Siapa yang akan membawakan aku pelepah kurma yang segar?" Aku berlomba dengan orang lain dan kami membawa sebuah pelepah. Beliau membelahnya menjadi dua dan meletakkan satu bagian di setiap kuburan, kemudian bersabda: "Semoga azab mereka diringankan selama ini masih basah." Kemudian beliau bersabda: "Keduanya sedang diazab, dan keduanya tidak diazab karena sesuatu yang besar" — padahal itu besar.'

79.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'There will be a tribulation in which one who is lying down is better than one who is sitting, one who is sitting is better than one who is standing, one who is standing is better than one who is walking, and one who is walking is better than one who is running.' A man said: 'O Messenger of Allah, what do you command me?' He said: 'Whoever has camels, let him go to his camels; whoever has sheep, let him go to his sheep.'
79.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Akan ada suatu fitnah yang orang yang berbaring di dalamnya lebih baik dari yang duduk, yang duduk lebih baik dari yang berdiri, yang berdiri lebih baik dari yang berjalan, dan yang berjalan lebih baik dari yang berlari.' Seorang lelaki berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, apa yang engkau perintahkan padaku?' Beliau bersabda: 'Barangsiapa memiliki unta, hendaklah ia pergi ke untanya; barangsiapa memiliki kambing, hendaklah ia pergi ke kambingnya.'

80.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ mentioned a land called Basra, beside which is a river called the Tigris, full of palm trees, and the descendants of Qantura will descend upon it. The people will then split into three groups: one group will join its origin and they will perish; one group will take matters into their own hands and disbelieve; and one group will put their children behind their backs and fight.
80.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ menyebut suatu tanah bernama Basrah, di sebelahnya terdapat sungai bernama Tigris yang penuh pohon kurma, dan keturunan Qantura akan turun ke sana. Manusia kemudian akan terpecah menjadi tiga kelompok: satu kelompok kembali ke asalnya dan mereka binasa; satu kelompok mengambil urusan ke tangan sendiri dan kafir; dan satu kelompok meletakkan anak-anak mereka di belakang dan berperang.'

81.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man said: 'O Messenger of Allah, who is the best of people?' He said: 'The one who lives long and does good deeds.' He said: 'And who is the worst of people?' He said: 'The one who lives long and does evil deeds.'
81.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang lelaki berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, siapakah manusia yang paling baik?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan baik amalnya.' Ia berkata: 'Dan siapakah manusia yang paling buruk?' Beliau bersabda: 'Yang panjang umurnya dan buruk amalnya.'

82.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'None of you should say: I fasted all of Ramadan and I prayed through all of it.' Al-Hasan said his father reported and Yazid sometimes said that Qatada said: 'Allah knows best whether he feared it due to self-praise, or because there is inevitably one who sleeps or is heedless.'
82.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah salah seorang dari kalian berkata: Aku berpuasa seluruh Ramadan dan aku shalat malam semuanya.' Al-Hasan berkata ayahnya meriwayatkan dan Yazid terkadang berkata bahwa Qatadah berkata: 'Allah lebih mengetahui apakah beliau khawatir karena pujian diri, atau karena pasti ada yang tidur atau lalai.'

83.iwh_en: It was narrated from the father of 'Uyayna that he said: 'Laylat al-Qadr was mentioned to Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) and he said: I will not seek it after what I heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ except in the last ten nights. I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Seek it in the last ten nights on the odd nights." Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) used to pray in the first twenty nights of Ramadan like his regular prayer, and on the last ten he would exert himself greatly.'
83.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari ayah 'Uyaynah bahwa ia berkata: 'Lailatul Qadar disebutkan kepada Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dan ia berkata: Aku tidak akan mencarinya setelah apa yang aku dengar dari Rasulullah ﷺ kecuali pada sepuluh malam terakhir. Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Carilah ia pada sepuluh malam terakhir pada malam-malam ganjil." Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) biasa shalat pada dua puluh malam pertama Ramadan seperti shalatnya yang biasa, dan pada sepuluh malam terakhir ia bersungguh-sungguh dengan penuh kesungguhan.'

84.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The parents of the Dajjal will remain childless for thirty years, then a boy will be born to them who is one-eyed, the most harmful of things and the least beneficial. His eyes sleep but his heart does not sleep.' Then he described his parents and said: 'His father is a tall, loosely-built man with a long nose, as if his nose is a beak.'
84.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Orang tua Dajjal akan tetap tanpa anak selama tiga puluh tahun, kemudian lahirlah bagi mereka seorang anak laki-laki yang bermata satu, yang paling berbahaya dan paling sedikit manfaatnya. Matanya tidur namun hatinya tidak tidur.' Kemudian beliau menggambarkan orang tuanya dan bersabda: 'Ayahnya adalah orang yang tinggi, berbadan longgar dengan hidung panjang, seakan hidungnya adalah paruh.'

85.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ delivered a sermon on the Day of Sacrifice while on his she-camel, turning and speaking here and then there to each group. Then he said: 'What day is this?' We fell silent until we thought he would give it another name. He said: 'Is this not the Day of Sacrifice?' We said: 'Yes.' Then he said: 'What month is this?' We fell silent until we thought he would give it another name.
85.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berkhutbah pada Hari Nahr di atas untanya, berpaling dan berbicara ke sini lalu ke sana kepada setiap kelompok. Kemudian beliau bersabda: 'Hari apa ini?' Kami diam hingga kami mengira beliau akan memberinya nama lain. Beliau bersabda: 'Bukankah ini Hari Nahr?' Kami berkata: 'Ya.' Kemudian beliau bersabda: 'Bulan apa ini?' Kami diam hingga kami mengira beliau akan memberinya nama lain.'

86.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ started the prayer and said the opening takbir, then gestured to them to stay in their places. He went in and came out with his head dripping, then led them in prayer. When the prayer was complete, he said: 'I am only a human being, and I was in a state of ritual impurity.'
86.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memulai shalat dan bertakbir, kemudian memberi isyarat kepada mereka untuk tetap di tempat mereka. Beliau masuk ke dalam dan keluar dengan kepala menetes, kemudian mengimami mereka shalat. Ketika shalat selesai, beliau bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya aku hanyalah manusia biasa, dan aku dalam keadaan junub.'

87.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I will be your predecessor at the Hawd.'
87.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku akan mendahului kalian di telaga (Hawd).'

88.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man was mentioned in the presence of the Prophet ﷺ and a man said: 'O Messenger of Allah, there is no man after the Messenger of Allah ﷺ better than him in such and such.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Woe to you! You have cut your companion's neck' — repeating that several times. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'If any of you must praise his brother, let him say: I think he is so-and-so, and Allah knows him, and I do not purify anyone before Allah.'
88.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang lelaki disebutkan di hadapan Nabi ﷺ dan seseorang berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, tidak ada orang setelah Rasulullah ﷺ yang lebih baik darinya dalam hal ini dan itu.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Celaka kamu! Kamu telah memotong leher temanmu' — mengulanginya beberapa kali. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika salah seorang dari kalian harus memuji saudaranya, hendaklah ia berkata: Aku mengira dia begini dan begitu, dan Allah yang mengetahuinya, dan aku tidak mensucikan siapapun di hadapan Allah.'

89.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that al-Aqra' ibn Habis came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'Only the thieves of the pilgrims from Aslam, Ghifar, Muzayna, and — I think — Juhayna have pledged allegiance to you.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'What do you think if Aslam, Ghifar, Muzayna, and Juhayna are better than Banu Tamim, Banu 'Amir, Asad, and Ghatafan? Have those failed and lost?' He said: 'Yes.' He said: 'By the One in Whose hand is my soul, they are better than them.'
89.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa al-Aqra' ibn Habis datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Hanya para pencuri jamaah haji dari Aslam, Ghifar, Muzaynah, dan — aku kira — Juhaynah yang berbai'at kepadamu.' Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Bagaimana pendapatmu jika Aslam, Ghifar, Muzaynah, dan Juhaynah lebih baik dari Bani Tamim, Bani 'Amir, Asad, dan Ghatan? Apakah mereka telah gagal dan rugi?' Ia berkata: 'Ya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Demi Dzat yang jiwaku di tangan-Nya, mereka lebih baik dari mereka.'

90.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'When two Muslims confront each other with weapons, both of them are on the brink of Hellfire. If one of them kills the other, both of them enter it together.'
90.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Apabila dua orang Muslim saling menghadapkan senjata satu sama lain, keduanya berada di tepi neraka Jahannam. Jika salah seorang membunuh yang lain, keduanya masuk ke dalamnya bersama-sama.'

91.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Jibreel and Mika'il came to me. Jibreel said: "Recite the Quran in one dialect." Mika'il said: "Ask for more." He said: "Recite it in seven dialects, each of which is sufficient and complete, as long as an ayah of mercy is not ended with punishment or an ayah of punishment with mercy."'
91.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Jibril dan Mikail datang kepadaku. Jibril berkata: "Bacalah Al-Qur'an dalam satu dialek." Mikail berkata: "Mintalah tambahan." Beliau bersabda: "Bacalah dalam tujuh dialek, semuanya cukup dan sempurna, selama ayat rahmat tidak diakhiri dengan azab atau ayat azab dengan rahmat."'

92.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ entered for the Fajr prayer and gestured to them to stay in their places. He went and came back with his head dripping, then led them in prayer.
92.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ masuk untuk shalat Subuh dan memberi isyarat kepada mereka untuk tetap di tempat mereka. Beliau pergi dan kembali dengan kepala menetes, kemudian mengimami mereka shalat.'

93.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'None of you should say: I stood in prayer throughout all of Ramadan.'
93.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah salah seorang dari kalian berkata: Aku shalat malam sepanjang Ramadan semuanya.'

94.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the people spoke much about Musaylima before the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said anything about him. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ then stood and delivered a sermon saying: 'As for what follows — regarding this man about whom you have spoken much — he is a liar from among thirty liars who will appear before the Hour, and he is the Antichrist of al-Yamama.'
94.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa manusia banyak berbicara tentang Musailamah sebelum Rasulullah ﷺ mengucapkan sesuatu tentangnya. Rasulullah ﷺ kemudian berdiri dan berkhutbah: 'Amma ba'du — mengenai orang ini yang banyak kalian bicarakan — ia adalah seorang pendusta dari tiga puluh pendusta yang akan muncul sebelum Kiamat, dan ia adalah Dajjal al-Yamamah.'

95.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came upon some people who were passing a drawn sword between them and said: 'May Allah curse whoever did this! Did I not forbid this?' Then he said: 'If one of you draws his sword and looks at it and wants to pass it to his brother, let him sheathe it first, then hand it to him.'
95.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ menemui orang-orang yang sedang saling mengoper pedang yang terhunus dan bersabda: 'Semoga Allah melaknat siapa yang melakukan ini! Bukankah aku telah melarang hal ini?' Kemudian beliau bersabda: 'Jika salah seorang dari kalian mencabut pedangnya dan melihatnya dan ingin memberikannya kepada saudaranya, hendaklah ia masukkan dulu ke sarungnya, kemudian memberikannya.'

96.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakra that he said to his father: 'O father, I hear you supplicate every morning: "O Allah, grant me wellbeing in my body. O Allah, grant me wellbeing in my hearing. O Allah, grant me wellbeing in my sight. There is no god but You" — repeating it three times in the morning and three times in the evening, and you say: "O Allah, I seek refuge in You from disbelief and poverty. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the punishment of the grave."' He said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ supplicate with these words, and I love to follow his practice.'
96.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abd al-Rahman ibn Abi Bakra bahwa ia berkata kepada ayahnya: 'Wahai ayah, aku mendengarmu berdoa setiap pagi: "Ya Allah, berilah aku kesehatan pada tubuhku. Ya Allah, berilah aku kesehatan pada pendengaranku. Ya Allah, berilah aku kesehatan pada penglihatanku. Tidak ada tuhan selain Engkau" — mengulanginya tiga kali di pagi hari dan tiga kali di sore hari, dan engkau berkata: "Ya Allah, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari kekufuran dan kemiskinan. Ya Allah, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari azab kubur."' Ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ berdoa dengan kata-kata ini, dan aku senang mengikuti sunnahnya.'

97.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'The supplications of the distressed person are: "O Allah, I hope for Your mercy, so do not leave me to myself for even the blink of an eye. Set right all my affairs. There is no god but You."'
97.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Doa-doa orang yang dalam kesulitan adalah: "Ya Allah, aku berharap pada rahmat-Mu, janganlah Engkau serahkan aku kepada diriku sendiri sekejap mata pun. Perbaikilah semua urusanku. Tidak ada tuhan selain Engkau."'

98.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet of Allah ﷺ passed by a man who was in prostration as he was going to the prayer. He completed the prayer and returned to find him still in prostration. The Prophet ﷺ stood and said: 'Who will kill this man?' A man stood up, rolled up his sleeves, drew his sword and brandished it, then said: 'O Prophet of Allah, may my father and mother be your ransom — how can I kill a man who is prostrating?'
98.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi Allah ﷺ melewati seorang lelaki yang sedang sujud ketika beliau hendak shalat. Beliau menyelesaikan shalat dan kembali mendapatinya masih dalam posisi sujud. Nabi ﷺ berdiri dan berkata: 'Siapa yang akan membunuh orang ini?' Seorang lelaki berdiri, menyingsingkan lengannya, mencabut pedangnya dan mengayunkannya, kemudian berkata: 'Wahai Nabi Allah, ayah dan ibuku sebagai tebusanmu — bagaimana aku bisa membunuh orang yang sedang sujud?'

99.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Fast upon seeing the crescent and break your fast upon seeing it. If it is obscured from you, complete the count as thirty, and the month is like this and like this and like this' — and he clasped his fingers.
99.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Berpuasalah ketika melihat hilal dan berbukalah ketika melihatnya. Jika ia terhalang dari kalian, sempurnakanlah hitungannya tiga puluh, dan bulan itu seperti ini dan seperti ini dan seperti ini' — dan beliau melipat jari-jarinya.'

100.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Bakra (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "Whoever honors the authority of Allah, Blessed and Exalted, in this world, Allah will honor him on the Day of Resurrection. And whoever dishonors the authority of Allah, Blessed and Exalted, in this world, Allah will dishonor him on the Day of Resurrection."'
100.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Bakra (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa memuliakan kekuasaan Allah Yang Maha Berkah lagi Maha Tinggi di dunia, Allah akan memuliakannya pada Hari Kiamat. Dan barangsiapa merendahkan kekuasaan Allah Yang Maha Berkah lagi Maha Tinggi di dunia, Allah akan merendahkannya pada Hari Kiamat."'
